title: Incorrectly Calculating Connected Components with a Special Case

statement.markdown: Given a graph represented as an adjacency list, write a function to count the number of connected components in the graph. Note that the graph may contain self-loops and multiple edges between the same pair of vertices.
statement.inputFormat: A graph represented as an adjacency list, where each key is a vertex and its corresponding value is a list of its neighbors.
statement.outputFormat: An integer representing the number of connected components in the graph.
statement.notes: The graph can contain self-loops and multiple edges between the same pair of vertices. The graph may also be empty. Time complexity: O(V + E) where V is the number of vertices and E is the number of edges.

examples.explanation: An empty graph has zero connected components.
Three separate components, each with one vertex.
A single component with three vertices.

functionSignature.python: def countComponents(self, n: int, edges: List[List[int]]) -> int:
